answer:
The Linear API supports two primary authentication methods: personal API keys and OAuth 2.0 [1][2].

For personal scripts, use an API key obtained from your account security settings [1][3]. Pass this key in the Authorization header without the Bearer prefix [1][4].

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: <YOUR_API_KEY>" \
  --data '{ "query": "{ issues { nodes { id title } } }" }'
```

For applications built for others, use OAuth 2.0 [1][5]. After completing the authorization flow to obtain an access token, provide it in the Authorization header using the Bearer scheme [1][5][4].

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>" \
  --data '{ "query": "{ issues { nodes { id title } } }" }'
```

If using the official Linear TypeScript SDK, you can authenticate by passing your key or token directly to the client constructor [6].

results.title: Linear API Key: How to Generate and Use It (GraphQL Guide for Developers) | Unified.to
